title: Procurement Specialist
description:
We are seeking a highly organized and detail-oriented individual to join our team as a Procurement Specialist. In this role, you will be responsible for overseeing the purchasing process and ensuring that all goods and services are procured efficiently and cost-effectively. The Procurement Specialist will play a crucial role in maintaining positive relationships with suppliers and ensuring that all procurement activities comply with company policies and regulations.
ResponsibilitiesConduct market research to identify potential suppliers and evaluate their suitability for our organization's needs.Collaborate with internal stakeholders to determine procurement priorities and develop strategies for sourcing goods and services.Obtain and review price quotes from various suppliers to ensure competitive pricing.Negotiate contracts and terms with suppliers to secure favorable pricing and delivery conditions.Manage the procurement process, including issuing purchase orders, tracking deliveries, and resolving any issues or discrepancies.Monitor inventory levels and forecast future demand to ensure timely procurement of goods and services.Review and analyze supplier performance to identify opportunities for improvement and cost savings.
RequirementsBachelor's degree in business administration, supply chain management, or a related field.Proven experience working in procurement or supply chain management.Strong knowledge of procurement principles, practices, and regulations.Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.Proficient in using procurement software and MS Office Suite.Strong negotiation and communication skills.Ability to work well under pressure and meet tight deadlines.Detail-oriented with strong organizational skills.

title: Senior Project Manager - Medical Device R&D
description:
Job Title
Senior Project Manager - Medical Device R&D

Responsible for End-to-End leading of a project - from the initial start until the introduction in the Market, or internal delivery, by gaining agreements on and achieving project scope and objectives while balancing schedule, cost, quality, requirements and keeping abreast of developments in project management tools and processes as well as technical domain. title: Retail Sales Associate, Old Orchard - Full Time
description:
Please note – currently this role may not require an in-person interview and if you are selected for this role you will receive a job offer via email. Please watch your email closely for updates from us!

Are you ready to bring your amazing self to work?

At Macy's, Inc., we're on a mission to create a brighter future with bold representation for all. This is our Mission Every One. We know that each person here is unique. We respect and invest in each individual to create growth, pride and satisfaction. If we are able to bring our whole selves to work, it translates into a more abundant and wider array of ideas and energy for all to benefit from. Our success will be built on amazing colleagues, working together.

Job Summary

As a Retail Sales Associate, also known as a Generalist colleague, you are an integral part of bringing the Macy’s magic, and the Macy’s shopper is your highest priority. Your close attention to customer preferences and the expertise you develop in Macy’s merchandise and services will let you create exceptional experiences for every customer you encounter. As you help your team meet their sales goals through delivering consistently superior service, your positive, welcoming presence will be felt throughout your area. From the sales floor to the fitting rooms to product presentations, you will be part of a fun, inclusive team that rises to every challenge.

What We Can Offer You

An inclusive, challenging, and refreshingly fun work environment Empowerment to perform impactful work with tangible results Coverage across medical, dental, vision, and retirement Employee Assistance Program (mental health and financial literacy resources) Fully-Funded education benefits may include 100% of tuition, books, and fees Merchandise discounts Colleague Resource Groups (CRGs), give-back/volunteer opportunities

What You Will Do

Customer Service: Master store products, services, loyalty programs, and Macy’s sales systems and use knowledge to help customers find what they need and suggest additional items.Sales Support: Develop customer relationships to grow business and expand our client base. Respond to customer requests for additional merchandise or other assistance, including sharing how to use an electronic device to shop and compare. Assist in locating and pulling merchandise to fulfill digital customer orders.Merchandise Organization: Maintain department recovery standards, including selling floor and fitting rooms.Update Pricing and Signage: Deliver optimal merchandise execution presentation, ensure accurate merchandise pricing, and process markdowns in a timely and accurate manner.

Who You Are

Focused on upholding Macy’s dedication to fulfilling ideals of diversity, inclusion and respect that our company aspires to achieve, every day in every store and office, with every customer and associateFlexible availability, including days, evenings, weekends and holidays with regular, dependable attendance and punctualityEnjoy meeting people, learning about them, and sharing information Resourceful and eager to start a new venture and can adapt to changing priorities; you can work on your own but are great with team dynamicsYou can handle electronic devices with no problemNo Education or Experience Required

Essential Physical Requirements

Prolonged periods of standing/walking around the store or department Reach with hands and arms, use hands to press, handle, and feelOccasionally required to lift and/or move up to 25 lbs., climb ladders, stoop, kneel, crouch, and crawlRequires close vision, color vision, and ability to adjust focus

About Macy’s

Now is an exciting time for a Macy’s career as we continue to focus on creating exciting and memorable experiences for every Macy’s customer, whether their journey starts online or in the store. Our opportunities in Sales, Merchandise, Operations, Customer Service, Warehouse, Marketing, and other areas let you express your creativity and commitment to excellence, grow your career, and make a difference to your Macy’s colleagues and customers.

Bring your creativity, energy, and ideas to the Macy’s team – Apply Today!

title: Ecommerce Manager
description:
Fireside is an award winning digital consultancy focusing on driving revenue for high growth ecommerce brands, through intense focus on Conversion Rate Optimization, Customer Retention, and Email Marketing. We focus heavily on culture and community. If you are looking for a flexible work schedule, averaging approximately 25-35 hours per week, working remotely but also alongside a dedicated and collaborative team, then this role may be right for you!
This is the Right Fit for you if:You are highly comfortable analyzing websites, landing pages, and user funnels and you’re quick to identify areas of conversion opportunities.You are able to marry qualitative and quantitative metrics alongside UX best practices and knowledge of the competitive landscape to formulate strategic insights and actions to strategically optimize conversion rate for our clients.You roll up your sleeves to execute effectively, and iterate quickly with A/B testing and experimentation.You are a highly analytical person with a proven track record of driving and accelerating revenue growth to increase retention within customer segments.You’re passionate about ensuring our client’s customers become customers for life and are focused on increasing customer LTV.You have hands-on experience developing, planning, launching, and analyzing customer retention campaigns and are obsessed with customer lifecycle analytics.
You Also Love Working on:Formulating proposals based on data which could include: building a testing agenda, implementing quick wins or onboarding a new technology platform.Working closely with other teams such as the retention division leads to ensure optimization strategies encompass all facets of digital marketing efforts including onsite and email strategy.Monitoring live tests including reporting & analysis.Translating client deliverables to development partners and vice versa.Partnering with our clients to understand primary KPIs, goals and objectives.Leading client engagements with thought leadership, proactiveness and a hunger to drive success.Developing strategy and leading the execution of the Retention marketing and Conversion Rate Optimization programs across email, push, SMS, and CRM marketing initiatives.Partnering cross-functionally to execute personalized customer experiences and segmentation programs that drive customer engagement and repeat conversion.Defining customer segmentation, performing cohort analysis, measure and regularly.Identifying and proposing opportunities to optimize customer retention and loyalty programs for each client based on clear understanding of client goals and needs.Analyzing site metrics and sales information to identify opportunities to increase sales and site conversion.
Experience Required:5-7+ years in user experience role and/or lifecycle marketing role, including at least 2 years in an e-commerce, direct-to-consumer role.Ability to multi-task and tackle challenges that differ client to client, quickly and thoughtfully.You have a critical understanding of the levers and tools to use in GA4, Shopify and Klaviyo to help assess, define and optimize your strategic marketing plan.Familiarity with other analytics or attribution platforms like Daasity and Triple Whale are a plus. Ability to work with a diverse group of stakeholders.Strong data background and experience with brand strategy.

title: Insider Risk Analyst (Remote, MST & PST)
description:
#WeAreCrowdStrike and our mission is to stop breaches. As a global leader in cybersecurity, our team changed the game. Since our inception, our market leading cloud-native platform has offered unparalleled protection against the most sophisticated cyberattacks. We’re looking for people with limitless passion, a relentless focus on innovation and a fanatical commitment to the customer to join us in shaping the future of cybersecurity. Consistently recognized as a top workplace, CrowdStrike is committed to cultivating an inclusive, remote-first culture that offers people the autonomy and flexibility to balance the needs of work and life while taking their career to the next level. Interested in working for a company that sets the standard and leads with integrity? Join us on a mission that matters - one team, one fight.

About The Role

The Insider Risk Analyst is a member of the CrowdStrike Chief Security Officer’s Global Security team and will play a key role in the maturation of the Insider Risk Program (IRP). The IRP is seeking a creative, collaborative, and motivated security professional to enhance our security posture to better protect CrowdStrike from sophisticated threats. This position reports to the Manager, Global Security - Risk & Facility Security Officer and will be responsible for a wide range of functions to ensure the protection of sensitive CrowdStrike information. You will also support the company’s Industrial Security Program. The position will work closely with partners in Information Security, Human Resources, Legal, Information Technology, and other business units to support and improve the IRP.

CrowdStrike is looking for somebody with a passion for insider risk as well as for risk management, counterintelligence, and national security.

What You’ll Do

The primary responsibility of this position is to identify potential threats to CrowdStrike and its operations from internal and external actors while operationalizing strategies to mitigate those threats in order to protect CrowdStrike’s workforce, assets, customers, and reputation.

Analyze and brief identified risks and vulnerabilities to a variety of audiences Perform detailed and comprehensive investigations and summarize findingsDevelop and deploy mitigation strategies such as security awareness materials, newsletters, case studies, and other communicationsLead outreach and risk management efforts involving a variety of stakeholdersProvide support to the Industrial Security ProgramStay up-to-date on the latest industry and regulatory developments related to insider risk in order to foster their integration into CrowdStrike internal processes Support general Insider Risk and Global Security operations

What You'll Need

2-5 years of experience in an insider threat/risk mitigation, intelligence analysis, corporate security, government security, or similar roleExceptional written and oral communication skills, to include conducting employee interviews and writing reportsKnowledge of and experience with insider threat/risk best practicesStrong computer skills and familiarity with cybersecurity conceptsPractical experience advising team leaders on security policy and guidelines, including executive-level presentation skillsProficiency in open-source intelligence (OSINT) and threat intelligence toolsExcellent problem solving, critical thinking, and analytical skills with the ability to communicate concepts to all audiencesDemonstrated ability to manage projects and establish rapport while building relationships with team members across all levels of the organizationProven ability to work independently with motivation, efficiency, and flexibility as well as willingness to work occasional nights and weekendsUS Citizenship with the ability to obtain a US Government security clearance due to access requirements

Bonus Points

Experience in the USG, DOD, Intelligence, or defense contractor communityExperience conducting fraud, counterintelligence, and other investigations Knowledge of the NISPOM and DCSA regulationsPractical experience developing, implementing and administering security education, training and awarenessWorking knowledge of global legal and privacy regulationsExperience in a remote-first environment

Education

Bachelor’s degree in a related field or relevant military experience (years of experience may be substituted in lieu of a degree)Relevant certifications or training courses

This role will require the candidate to periodically undergo and pass additional background and fingerprint check(s) consistent with government customer requirements.

Benefits Of Working At CrowdStrike

Remote-first cultureMarket leader in compensation and equity awardsCompetitive vacation and flexible working arrangements Comprehensive and inclusive health benefitsPhysical and mental wellness programsPaid parental leave, including adoption A variety of professional development and mentorship opportunitiesOffices with stocked kitchens when you need to fuel innovation and collaboration

We are committed to fostering a culture of belonging where everyone feels seen, heard, valued for who they are and empowered to succeed. Our approach to cultivating a diverse, equitable, and inclusive culture is rooted in listening, learning and collective action. By embracing the diversity of our people, we achieve our best work and fuel innovation - generating the best possible outcomes for our customers and the communities they serve.

CrowdStrike is committed to maintaining an environment of Equal Opportunity and Affirmative Action. If you need reasonable accommodation to access the information provided on this website, please contact Recruiting@crowdstrike.com , for further assistance.

CrowdStrike participates in the E-Verify program.

Notice of E-Verify Participation

Right to Work

CrowdStrike, Inc. is committed to fair and equitable compensation practices. The base salary range for this position in the U.S. is $65,000 - $110,000 per year + variable/incentive compensation + equity + benefits. A candidate’s salary is determined by various factors including, but not limited to, relevant work experience, skills, certifications and location.

title: Registered Nurse (RN)- Outpatient -GI Nurse, Endoscopy Ambulatory Surgery Center, FT
description:
Inspire health. Serve with compassion. Be the difference.

Job Summary

Provides clinical direction for all aspects of patient care in an outpatient setting. Demonstrates clinical competency utilizing evidence based practices/research in all areas of the nursing process: assessment, planning, implementation, evaluation and outcomes. Incorporates knowledge of safety protocols, cost containment and quality improvement throughout the nursing process. Identifies patient, unit, and organizational priorities and incorporates into the nursing process.

Adheres to all compliance and policy and procedure requirements of the organization including, but not limited to: licensure renewal, assigned training, employee health screenings, time and attendance policy, dress code policy, patient confidentiality, infection control, medication administration. Demonstrates knowledge of regulatory agency requirements (e.g., AAAHC, JCAHO, DHEC). Provides care within the scope of the SC Nursing Practice Act.

The outpatient setting usually does not require staff to be scheduled for a significant amount of shift, weekend, holiday, and on-call work.

Bonus

This position is bonus eligible, follow this link for details.

Accountabilities

Assessment - Collects data and completes nursing assessment utilizing appropriate area and/or age specific standards/policies and tools. Completes appropriate forms. Assesses and recognizes subtle or actual changes in patients' status and patient response to intervention. Identifies nursing priorities for patient plan of care based on nursing assessment (and other data as appropriate). Validates these priorities with healthcare team members and documents appropriately. - 20%

Planning/Outcomes: - Develops an individual plan of care based on the age and assessed needs of the patient. Develops the plan in collaboration with the patient and/or family, the physician, and other members of the interdisciplinary health care team. Updates the plan based on changes in the patient's condition and communicates changes in the plan to other members of the team. Identifies realistic and measurable goals and outcomes to be attained prior to discharge. Validates these with the patient and/or family. Demonstrates respect for the patient's/family's cultural, spiritual, and belief system in planning for care. Includes planning for discharge to assure continuity of care and a smooth transition post discharge from one level of care to another (to include appropriate referrals). - 20%

Implementation - Provides direct care to assigned patients consistent with the established nursing plan. Performs specific tasks based on organizational, departmental and/or unit policies and procedures within the RN scope of practice. Delegates nursing tasks as appropriate to members of the healthcare team, developing written assignments (if appropriate) and ensures completion of assigned tasks. Monitors quality of care delivered on a continual basis; ensures appropriate surveillance of assigned patients. Provides care based on patient and unit priorities, coordinating and organizing tasks as appropriate, utilizing known resources internal and external to the unit. Incorporates cost containment principles into nursing care. Assumes responsibility for patient safety by maintaining a safe environment to include awareness of safety procedures, protocols and adherence to patient care standards. Utilizes appropriate technology in daily practice. Utilizes performance indicators to collect data, monitor quality and effectiveness while providing nursing care. Completes assigned hospital/unit performance improvement monitoring (audits). - 20%

Evaluation: - Evaluates nursing plan of care for effectiveness and determines progress toward expected outcomes. Revises diagnosis, outcomes and plan of care based on evaluation of data. Reports changes in patient status and refers unresolved problems to appropriate health team members. Discusses outcomes with peers via shift to shift report and with other healthcare team members as appropriate. title: Development Team Lead
description:
Banyan Software provides the best permanent home for successful enterprise software companies, their employees, and customers. We are on a mission to acquire, build and grow great enterprise software businesses all over the world that have dominant positions in niche vertical markets. In recent years, Banyan was named the #1 fastest-growing private software company in the US on the Inc. 5000 and amongst the top 10 fastest-growing companies by the Deloitte Technology Fast 500. Founded in 2016 with a permanent capital base setup to preserve the legacy of founders, Banyan focuses on a buy and hold for life strategy for growing software companies that serve specialized vertical markets.

Specializing in energy forecasting, pricing, trading, risk management, scheduling, settlement, reconciliation, and decision support functionalities, this company plays a crucial role in supporting today's load serving, energy marketing, and energy-consuming organizations.
Committed to tackling the energy market's digitalization, de-marginalization, and decentralization challenges, this portfolio company is at the forefront of driving industry transformation. With a market-differentiating product offering and a proven track record of delivering substantial value to its customers, it is poised for significant growth in the years ahead.
The company's strategic positioning within Banyan Software's diverse portfolio, coupled with the dynamic nature of the energy sector, underscores its potential to thrive amidst evolving market conditions.
Job DescriptionWe are seeking an experienced and highly motivated Development Team Lead for oursoftware development team. The Development Team Lead will play a critical role in shapingthe future of our SaaS products, driving innovation, and ensuring the successful delivery ofSaaS services. Experience in the Energy industry is a plus.
Job Responsibilities:- Plan, prioritize, and oversee the execution of software development projects anddeployment of SaaS services.- Allocate and manage development resources effectively, including personnel,equipment, and software tools.- Lead and mentor a small cohesive team of software developers, fostering a cultureof collaboration and continuous improvement.- Assist the development team in resolving technical challenges.- Stay current with software industry trends to guide technology decisions.- Provide technical leadership and guidance to the development team.- Continuously evaluate and improve development processes, methodologies, andtools to enhance efficiency and quality.- Develop energy industry knowledge to assist analysts in identifying how best toapply software solutions to energy industry requirements.

Experience leading a small cohesive team is a plus.- Demonstrated ability to drive results and meet deadlines.- Excellent project management and organizational skills.-Strong communication and interpersonal skills.- Strong technical background in C++, Java and a scripting language (such asPython).- Experience with Linux servers.- Experience with SQL and No-SQL databases is a plus.- Experience with a servlet engine (e.g. Tomcat, WebLogic, JBoss) is a plus.- Experience with a messaging engine (e.g. Kafka, MQ, JMS) is a plus.- Experience working in the Energy industry is a plus.

title: 10378 Store Manager
description:
Overview

SALLY STORE MANAGER:

By working at Sally Beauty, you would be part of the largest hair and beauty supplier in the world and we are on a mission to empower our customers to express themselves through hair – and we need passionate and talented people to make this happen!!

Are you a proven leader with a strong drive to succeed? Do you believe it is important to work hard but also have fun doing it? As a Store Manager, we want you to bring your leadership, talents, and experience to a team-oriented and dynamic environment. We believe that our success is based primarily on the advice and hard work of our valued team members to bring the Hair and Beauty world to our customers. Our managers are innovators, role models and coaches who drive results and the development of our team associates.

Your role as a Store Manager:

When it comes to the customer experience – it all starts with you. Your role is to manage talent, inspire your team and make sure every customer comes away with a great experience. It is up to you to ensure that each customer receives an unforgettable experience and a custom-fit solution for their hair and beauty needs. You will ensure that your store meets or exceeds sales goals by overseeing all store operations with an emphasis on maintaining assigned budget, controlling expenses, growing sales, and managing inventory. You will protect customers, associates, and store assets by ensuring that all staff follows safety and security rules and procedures, as well as Company policies.You will make sure your store always looks its best!


Why you’ll love working here:

The people are creative, fun and passionate about beauty.Generous product discount and free sample products.You will receive a great education regarding our products.You will have ample opportunity for growth.You may qualify for one or more of the following – medical, dental, vision, 401k, vacation, sick and holiday time depending on the average hours worked.


Requirements:

Qualifications to be a Store Manager:

At least 3 years of customer service/retail sales experience and 1 year of previous sales management experience.Previous experience in operational, financial and performance management.Cosmetology license is a plus – but not required.Must be 18 years of age or older.Passion for all things hair and beauty!


Legal wants you to know:

 Must be able to lift up to 25 lbs, occasionally while on a ladder, with or without accommodation. May be exposed to fumes and odors upon occasion.


Working Conditions/Physical Requirements

The position requires some physical exertion such as long periods of standing; walking; recurring bending, crouching, stooping, stretching, reaching, or similar activities; recurring lifting of moderately heavy items such as shipment or record boxes. The position involves working around moving parts, carts, or machines, and may occasionally include exposure to irritant chemicals.

Additional Information:

Interested in this exciting challenge and always be in the know about the latest trends in hair and beauty? Yes? Then we would love to hear from you.

“At Sally Beauty Holdings, we find beauty in diversity. Our inclusivity and self-expression are what fuel our innovation and growth. You are welcome here, and you can thrive here. We find beauty in YOU. Join us.”

Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to their race, color, religion, national origin, sex, or disability.

SBH, Inc. is an Equal Opportunity Employer. In compliance with federal law, all persons hired will be required to verify identity and eligibility to work in the United States and to complete the required employment eligibility verification form upon hire.

title: Research Technician III
company_name: University of Arizona College of Medicine - Phoenix
description:
The laboratory of Dr. Wondisford and Dr. Radovick in the College of Medicine-Phoenix is looking for an enthusiastic and motivated Research Technician III. Dr. Wondisford and Dr. Radovick recently joined the College of Medicine-Phoenix to continue their biomedical research projects funded by NIH. The laboratories are in the newly renovated Biomedical Science Partnership Building (BSPB) in the downtown of Phoenix along with new equipment and friendly crews.
Dr. Radovick is interested in the molecular mechanisms of Kisspeptin in hypothalamus to regulate sexual development and related metabolism.
Dr. Wondisford studies the molecular mechanisms of glucose homeostasis and thyroid hormones in metabolic disease such as diabetes and fatty liver disease.
Successful candidates will join a multidisciplinary team within the setting of an outstanding new facility with capabilities in imaging, flow cytometry, bioinformatics, high throughput screening, phosphoproteomics, and other advanced approaches. The position is part of a well-funded research program and will provide the opportunity to produce high-impact publications and establish a record of grant award funding. Enthusiastic applicants with experience in Biology, Biochemistry, Physiology, Chemistry are encouraged to apply.
This position is eligible for visa sponsorship.

title: Director - Events and Experiential Marketing
description:
Job Description

Description of Position

The Global Commercial and Digital Experience (CDX) team is a newly formed organization, serving as part of Global Marketing, Commercial Services and Technology Solutions (GMCS & TS). Working cross-functionally, the CDX team aims to enable the enterprise to disrupt the present and build the animal health of tomorrow by designing and delivering advanced commercial capabilities and ambitious digital experiences to help the business launch their next big bets and enable innovation at global scale. We partner across the enterprise to design, scale, enable and sustain capabilities and solutions that generate the greatest impact for the business and value for our customers. We are the critical mass required to support the enterprise across biopharma, technology and intelligence portfolios as they create moments that matter with customers to become the best animal health company in the world.

If you are a strategic visionary with a profound enterprise mindset and a passion for driving business growth through commercial and digital transformation, this role presents an extraordinary opportunity for your next career move.

We are looking for a dynamic and visionary Events and Experiential Marketing Director to develop and lead the vision for our global presence through impactful and strategic events. In this pivotal role, you will be the driving force behind our event marketing initiatives, fusing creativity with data-driven insights to amplify our brand and contribute to our growth. This role will report directly to the Executive Director, Customer Experience and Content Excellence.

They will be at the forefront of identifying, evaluating, and seizing new opportunities, leading the way in planning, executing, and meticulously measuring the success of diverse events. The primary mission is to curate experiences that not only capture attention but also generate high-quality leads. Collaborating closely with senior stakeholders, they will craft and implement strategies with the utmost success in lead generation and revenue growth.

The ideal candidate is a dynamic and experienced leader, with a strong focus on creating industry-leading physical and virtual customer experiences. The role demands meticulous attention to detail before, during, and after events to ensure a consistently exceptional customer experience and a cohesive animal health company presence. This individual will drive transformation by challenging conventional approaches and redefining future animal health experiences.

Primary Responsibilities

Strategic Planning:

Lead the orchestration and execution of a comprehensive global vision for events, and virtual experiential marketing, perfectly aligned with the company's overall commercial objectives and brand positioning as an integrated animal health company.Foster strategic customer loyalty through the successful planning and execution of events aimed at increasing visibility, generating sales leads, and retaining and attracting new customers.

Leadership

Inspire and lead a team of shared resources across meetings, events, and content capabilities, providing guidance, support, and mentorship to ensure successful event planning and execution.Promote visibility and transparency of all global, regional, and local meetings, events, and launch roadmaps and programs.Lead the meetings, events, and experiential marketing community of practice and workshops.Seek innovative ways to modernize and standardize global-to-local implementation and deployment of meetings, events, and launch programs.Ensure consistent implementation and operational capabilities across meetings and events.

Event Curation

Strategic planning, organization, and execution of a wide range of events, such as conferences, tradeshows, product launches, and experiential marketing activations.Direct the execution of both physical and virtual experiences, including internal meetings, industry/trade events, key opinion leader (KOL) meetings, thought leadership conferences, and new product launches/brand activations.

Performance Measurement & Competitive Analysis

Forecast, evaluate, measure, and report on the effectiveness of events, leads generated, and ROI.Establish key performance indicators (KPIs) and metrics to measure the success and impact of meetings, events, and experiential marketing initiatives. Provide regular reports and analysis to management on ROI, customer insights, and competitive landscape.Define and ensure that KPIs and metrics are planned for and tracked during and after event execution.Monitor the budget for global meetings, events, and experiential marketing, ensuring cost-effective planning and execution while delivering exceptional experiences.Track and monitor all budgets related to meetings, travel, and events, defining a measurement framework to assess return on investment (ROI). Provide guidance to brand or franchise owners throughout the process and ensure compliance with budgetary and regulatory requirements.

Vendor And Supplier Management & Performance Optimization

Identify and manage relationships with vendors, suppliers, and event production agencies to ensure the delivery of high-quality services and products within budget and timeline constraints.Direct all activities related to agencies and vendors, including the development of exhibit/virtual environments, program planning, and speaker/presentation management. Communicate and align stakeholders globally, regionally, and locally.

Influencing And Driving Transformation

Influence and collaborate closely with internal stakeholders at all levels, including Global Marketing, Regions, Commercial Leaders, CDX, IT, Communications, and Executive Leadership, to understand their objectives and incorporate them into strategies and plans.Influence and collaborate cross-functionally with Travel & Meetings Group, Communications, Procurement, Global, Regional, and Local Marketing & Subject Matter Experts (SMEs), CDX peers, IT, and Regional/Country Operations, as well as external strategic partnerships/agencies.Approach all projects with a marketing and strategic growth mindset, collaborating effectively with individuals at all levels of a matrix organization to execute integrated experiences that drive results.Align stakeholders at all levels of the organization on pre-event, during-event, and post-event engagement and lead creative content process.

Innovation & Trend Monitoring

Drive and scale experiential marketing capabilities within the organization, including the identification and evaluation of virtual platforms and emerging technologies.Stay current with the latest trends and innovations in meetings, events, and experiential marketing. Proactively identify opportunities to enhance the company's event experiences and brand presence.Identify and deploy new, innovative technologies that enhance meeting, event, and launch customer experiences.

About You

Executive Presence - you have the ability to simplify complex concepts and effectively influence senior leaders, aligning those concepts with their commercial goals and inspiring them to take action.Entrepreneurial - You thrive in ambiguous situations, easily adapt to change, and actively drive change within organizations.Growth Mindset - You have a passion for continuous learning, exploring new ideas, and consistently improving yourself, your team, and your projects.Data Driven - You make informed decisions by relying on data and possess a clear understanding of the data required to drive impactful outcomes.Tenacious - You embrace challenges and maintain a positive outlook in the face of setbacks, staying focused on your objectives.Activator - You possess the ability to initiate and complete tasks, utilizing your influential skills to bring together the right individuals and motivate them towards progress.Coach - You empower others to excel in their work by inspiring, motivating, and enabling them to align with your vision and take ownership of their actions.

Education

Bachelor’s degree in business administration, marketing, or related areas of study.

Required

Minimum of 10 years of experience in a senior role such as Marketing, Global meetings, events, experiential marketing or commercial operationsProven track record of planning and executing successful events and experiential marketing campaigns across physical, digital and virtual mediums. Ability to execute senior leader endorsed, multi-dimensional projects across a broad group of enterprise stakeholders.Experience in leading diverse teams to accelerate Global and Regional events and/or product launches with an understanding of the dynamics of a networked Global, Regional and local organization.Strategic understanding of emerging digital strategies, tools, and platforms.Excellent project management skills, with the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously and meet tight deadlines.Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to translate data into actionable insights.Strong negotiation and vendor management skills with experience in contract negotiations and budget management.Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams and influence without authority.Results oriented mindset with a focus on achieving targets and driving business outcomes. Ability to drive and thrive in a fast-paced, high-pressure environment with proven ability to think strategically, communicate clearly, think conceptually, deal with ambiguity, drive clarity for the team and work under pressure.Strong executive presence, facilitation, written communication and presentation skillsWillingness to travel internationally.

NOTICE FOR INTERNAL APPLICANTS

title: Director, Client Services - Home Based
description:
Description

At SCP Health, what you do matters

As part of the SCP Health team, you have an opportunity to make a difference. At our core, we work to bring hospitals and healers together in the pursuit of clinical effectiveness. With a portfolio of over 8 million patients, 7500 providers, 30 states, and 400 healthcare facilities, SCP Health is a leader in clinical practice management spanning the entire continuum of care, including emergency medicine, hospital medicine, wellness, telemedicine, intensive care, and ambulatory care.

Why You Will Love Working Here

Flexible schedules and work-from-home opportunities

Strong track record of providing excellent work/life balance

Comprehensive Benefits Package And Competitive Compensation

Commitment to fostering an inclusive culture of belonging and empowerment through our core values - collaboration, courage, agility, and respect.

What You’ll Be Doing

Client Services, is responsible for leading, directing, and coordinating all activities of the Management Client Services Team for SCP Health Revenue Cycle Services (SCP Health RCS). The Director, Client Services will be the Senior Leader and escalation point for Management Services Clients, RCS Client Services Managers, and RCS Domain Operations Teams for the resolution of any issues regarding Client coding, billing, and collection activities. The Director, Client Services will work with the Chief Revenue Officer, Vice Presidents, RCS PMO and Analytics Teams, as well as Client Services Managers to establish and maintain standards of service and integrity for client coding, billing, and collections programs, as well as assist in the development of new service opportunities, workflow designs and business development pursuits as needed.

Responsible for hiring, training, and managing a team of Client Services Managers. Since the Client Services Manager will be the face of SCP Health to our coding, billing, and collections Clients, you will coach and mentor the Client Services Managers with the appropriate skill set and soft skills to be that primary contact and the primary liaison with the Clients. Focus on building an energized Client-facing team who are professional, competent, and motivated. Work with Revenue Cycle Operations Teams to uphold the current Client coding, billing, and collections standards and continue to build and improve on the Client coding, billing, and collections service line.Interact with traditional Management Services delivery operations teams, as well as Group Operating teams in “optimization” client arrangements, to assure coordination and delineation of client-facing duties by broader SCP Health delivery teams. Perform business development duties to include pricing proposals, leading operational contract reviews, and maintain communications with RCS Leadership through client contract negotiation, execution and close. Maintain staffing ratios for Client Management Team to assure adequate coverage based on internal metrics (8-10 facilities/Client Services Manager) and develop sufficient budget considerations to meet annual RCS budget objectives for Management Services. Provide necessary inputs or additional new expenses or cost savings related to the Management Client Services domain. Provide regular report outs on all activities and issues related to Client coding, billing, and collections services and Client Services Managers activities. This report out should include metrics on the current health of the Client's coding, billing, and collections, operational issues, accomplishments, and risks, and client communications (meetings, onsite visits, etc.)Coordinate with Analytics and other RCS domains to produce the Monthly Operating Review of the Client coding, billing, and collections service line report out to present to RCS Executive Leadership and RCS Domain Leaders. Maintain Client relationships and lead your team to optimize clients' revenue cycle performance for each client site while maintaining the highest level of compliance with governmental and industry standards of conduct, in accordance with SCP Health's mission, core values, and goals. Oversee Client Services Managers' client post go-live reviews to identify any operational challenges and resolve start up issues, then utilize that information to improve client on-boarding processes.Review the Client Services Managers meeting notes and weekly status updates to identify any issues that may need to be escalated and resolved promptly. Work with the Client Services Managers to review client financials and monitor reimbursement, cash flow, fee schedules, and billing effectiveness. Together with the Client Services Managers, analyze trends and coordinate with appropriate departments to troubleshoot or optimize revenue cycle services performance. Assure overall client performance based on Service Level Agreements and program economics. Perform ongoing audits of Accounting's invoice reports to ensure refunds and payments are appropriately attributed and client invoices for services are appropriately calculated. Develop a solid understanding of SCP Health RCS processes and systems to enable issue resolution and effective, accurate communication to the client. Schedule and conduct site visits to client facilities, as needed. Meet with and present key personnel including facility administration, the Medical Director, CEO, CFO, COO, department heads and staff responsible for data and chart delivery. Inclusive of all duties listed above, but not exclusive of other duties as determined necessary for the success of the Director, Client Services objectives. 

Who/What We Are Looking For

Bachelor’s Degree RequiredMaster’s Degree (MBA, MHA, or comparable Master’s Degree) Preferable10 years of Revenue Cycle Services experience5 years of experience in a leadership and managerial role5 years of Client Services experience in a Healthcare environmentProject Manager Professional (PMP) Certification preferredLean 6 Sigma Certification preferred.

To demonstrate compliance with SCP Health’s vaccination policy, proof of COVID-19 vaccination status will be requested upon a conditional offer of employment for onsite positions at a client company/healthcare facility. SCP health will consider religious and/or medical/disability accommodation and other legally required exemption request as required by applicable law.

description:

We are currently looking for a Director of Product, Platform Security to lead our Platform Security portfolio to deliver secure products, frameworks, and features for use by both internal and external clients. title: Director, Category Management
description:
PLZ is committed to product stewardship and innovation, strong ethical standards, sustainable operations and most importantly, the development of our people. Whether at our manufacturing facilities or corporate headquarters, PLZ prioritizes health and safety, and offers you the support you need to create the career you want, through engagement, equity and inclusion, and opportunity.
From household cleaning solutions to personal care products, and food to automotive lubricants, PLZ produces more than 2,500 different aerosol and liquid products in our facilities across the continent. We know that being the partner of choice for our clients starts with being the partner of choice for our employees. Learn more at www.plzcorp.com/careers or visit https://youtu.be/XYNIFpLQIJY
The Director, Category Management for PLZ’s Specialty Solutions (SPS) business unit will lead strategic market planning within our core product categories with the objective of driving market share growth while fostering more meaningful relationships with our valued customers. This role will function as a critical link between the SPS sales team and its customers by turning insights and strategies into actions that create sustainable, profitable growth for the business. This is a highly cross-functional role interfacing with a broad array of internal teams, as well as senior executives within both PLZ and its customers.
Key Responsibilities:Industry ResearchConduct industry research through data analysis, intelligence gathering, and store walks to inform SPS leadership team on the state of core categories in the marketplace.Identify key industry trends across customers, suppliers, competitors, and regulators.Develop data-driven insights to drive growth and pricing strategies within each core category.Serve as the category expert and thought partner to the SPS leadership team within core product categories.
Business DevelopmentCraft PLZ’s sales narrative within each focus category. Develop messaging that serves as the keystone for internal and external sales collateral and presentations.Create category and customer specific presentations to endorse PLZ capabilities, product offerings, and communicate its value proposition to the customer.Participate in customer meetings to drive new product development in core categories and logical adjacencies. Translate customer needs into an actionable product pipeline.Work closely with PLZ’s Research & Development organization teams to build a differentiated and compelling product set which solves critical pain points and responds to the “voice of the customer.”Manage product development lifecycle in core categories to drive new business growth.
Category ManagementDevelop monthly reports to analyze category performance and guide monthly business planning.Maintain competitive intelligence database for core Specialty Solutions categories. Translate this information into actionable insights for senior leadership.Drive ongoing simplification of PLZ’s portfolio within core categories.
Qualifications required: 10+ years of experience in category, brand and product management focused on retail brands and/or contract manufacturing preferred.Results-oriented, data-driven mind-set, with a strong business acumen possessing impeccable attention to detail.Experience working with IRI, Circana and NPD with a proven ability to turn data into actionable insights.Critical thinker with problem-solving skills.Ability to display strong technical, interpersonal, and analytical skills and influence cross-functional team members and leadership to drive change.Strong understanding of financial principles and ability to analyze profitability and sales data to drive decision making and strategies.Excellent communication and presentation skills to effectively communicate category strategies and plans to stakeholders.Proficiency in using data analysis tools and software, such as Microsoft Excel, Tableau, Power BI to analyze and interpret sales data.Ability to work in a fast-paced, dynamic environment and adapt to changing market conditions.Ability to travel as required for customer meetings or industry events.
PLZ Corp offers a robust suite of benefits including:Medical, Dental & Vision benefits.Company paid Life and Short-Term disability.Voluntary benefits including additional life, disability, and hospital/accident insurance.401k & company provided match.Paid Time OffBonus incentive programs (Note, bonuses are subject to the rules governing the program, whereby an award, if any, depends on various factors, including, without limitation, individual and organizational performance.) All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, protected veteran status or other protected status. title: Head of Engineering
description:
A biotech company based in Berkeley, CA focused on biological research to drive novel therapeutics discoveries is seeking a Head of Engineering with a background in machine learning, molecular biology, and/or biochemistry to join their growing team.
The Opportunity: As Head of Engineering you will drive strategy across all engineering projects by overseeing the software and experimental engineering teams to enable and accelerate use of internal platform tools in the start-up studio. You will speed up the company's ability to strategically narrow the biological search space by advising on and working through engineering bottlenecks, with an eye on scalability. You will also drive the development of an internal software product that allows our scientists to quickly use platform tools to make decisions and track outcomes.
Key Contributions:Set strategic engineering project goals with the CEOBe a part of the leadership team making joint decisions on growth, finance, and operationsOversee the entire engineering process to build useful, fast, scalable versions of the company’s unique platform tools.Engineer workflows and tools to increase throughput and predictability of the core inference platformOversee software engineering team to productionize tools for broad usability with an eye on UI and UX designOversee experimental engineering team to develop and automate cutting edge molecular and imaging-based assays for new startups incubated in houseUtilize ML/AI to scale and improve the company's platform predictionsBuild and lead the Engineering teamsDrive the culture of the Engineering function to be the the company's flavor of entrepreneurship, science, and collaboration
Desired Experience:PhD in computer science, molecular biology, or other life sciences is preferable10+ years of experience across academia and biotech industry including engineering team or product development leadership within a start-up environmentStrong software development expertise at scale with a background in data science, machine learning, and/or artificial intelligenceAbility to think strategically about connecting the computational and experimental engineering teams' work with an eye on end user & product use caseThe ideal candidate will have engineering experience within the biotech industry (i.e. using genomic/clinical data to develop predictive therapeutics, looking at sequencing data, using AI to predict drug targets, or building predictive platforms or tools to support research and drug development)Experience taking a product from concept to marketUnderstanding of both computational and lab experimental toolsUnderstanding of biology so they can map tools to scientific decision making process in biological researchExperience taking a scientific/research idea through the product life cycle, from concept to marketAn experienced people manager; able to meet people where they are in their learning journey
The position will require the individual to be on-site at the Berkeley, California headquarters.

Skilled in holding people accountable and developing their abilities to do their jobs.Strong business acumen, creativity, and problem solving skills combined with a bias for action.Tactful collaboration and relationship building skills with an ability to prioritize, negotiate, and work effectively with all levels of the organization.Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to listen and empathize with others and anticipate, respond, and pivot to the needs of the employees and business within a multi-cultural organization.Articulate communicator with polished written, verbal, and presentation skills.Demonstrated ability to handle confidential information with discretion.Strong process orientation and project management skills with the ability to delegate successfully and manage ambiguity objectivity.Strong knowledge of Microsoft Office (i.e. Outlook, Word, Excel, PowerPoint).

title: Chief Operating Officer, Property Management
description:
Korn Ferry has partnered with our client on their search for the role, Chief Operating Officer, Property Management.
Location: Philadelphia
Purpose of the Position:Our client is presently seeking a Chief Operating Officer, Property Management. This executive will assume responsibility for overseeing property management across all properties. This individual’s primary objectives will include ensuring the fiscal integrity of building operations, meeting financial performance targets, and maintaining high standards of property management. Additionally, this individual will ensure compliance with all relevant laws, regulations, policies, and procedures. Moreover, as a senior leader within the organization, this individual will serve as a culture champion, actively promoting the company’s mission and philosophy.
Key Responsibilities:With a data-driven approach to management, the COO provides direct supervision to the RVPs in Property Management Operations and Service Operations. This supervision ensures that the operations and maintenance of all properties align with overarching standards emphasizing quality, occupancy, cost control, and revenue objectives.
The individual actively:Maintains assigned properties in a decent, safe, and sanitary condition at all timesOrganizes assigned work efficientlyDevelops effective work methods that comply with expectationsPartners with internal and external resources to maintain high levels of property performance and standards:Interprets program standards, procedures, and regulations crucial for program effectivenessManages the budget for all area programsRecommends modifications to meet evolving program requirements and staff adjustmentsParticipates in developing the annual budget submission related to Property Management OperationsEnsures the area of responsibility operates within the established budgetAdvises the CEO and relevant C-level peers on pertinent budget, policy, program, and procedural mattersProvides updates on all phases of area programsRepresents organization partners at various public/business, community, and resident meetings
